meta.author: Adam 'MOOP' Johns — NASM CPT, CNC, WLS, PES
meta.description: MOOP's personal supplement reference — the stack I actually use, sourced from years of training, NASM education, and real blood-work-informed decisions. Not a prescription. Not generic advice. Just what works for a 47-year-old Marine veteran chasing strength, visceral fat loss, and longevity.
meta.disclaimer: These statements have not been evaluated by the Food and Drug Administration. These products are not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease. Consult your physician before starting any new supplement regimen.
meta.affiliate_disclosure: Some links on this page are Amazon Associate links. As an Amazon Associate I earn from qualifying purchases — at no extra cost to you.

core.label: Core Daily
core.description: Non-negotiable. I take these every single day.

t_stack.label: T-Optimization
t_stack.description: Testosterone + hormone-support protocol. Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri/Sat only. Off Wed + Sun.

workout.label: Workout Days
workout.description: Tue + Fri (fit20 days). Pre and post-session support.

cycling.label: Cycling / Rotational
cycling.description: Rotated in on specific days for targeted benefit. Not daily.

avoid.label: Don't Waste Your Money
avoid.description: I tried these, the research doesn't hold up for my goals, or the dosing/cost is wrong. Removed from my stack.

supplements.name: Vitamin D3 + K2 (MK-7)
supplements.timing: AM with fat (fat-soluble — take with eggs, olive oil, or avocado)
supplements.dose: D3: 4,000 IU / K2: 100–200 mcg MK-7 form
supplements.my_protocol: Every morning with my first meal. Non-negotiable — especially at 47 and in the mid-Atlantic where sun exposure is inconsistent. K2 directs calcium away from arteries and into bones, so if you're taking D3 without K2 you may be doing more harm than good.
supplements.why_i_take_it: D3 deficiency is epidemic in men over 40, especially veterans who spent years in garrison or shipboard environments. It's foundational for testosterone, immune function, and mood.

brands.name: Melaleuca Peak Performance Pack
brands.note: Included in the pack — covered if you're already subscribed.

brands.name: Sports Research D3 + K2
brands.note: Best standalone value. Organic coconut oil for absorption.

supplements.coach_note: Fat-soluble — if you're taking this in a fasted state you're probably absorbing 40-50% less than you should be. Eat first.
supplements.doc_note: His 2018 labs showed no D level tested — most likely deficient. At 47, optimal range is 60–80 ng/mL, not the 30 ng/mL 'normal' threshold. MK-7 form of K2 has a longer half-life than MK-4. Don't skimp on this one.

supplements.name: Magnesium Glycinate
supplements.my_protocol: Every night. I use Mela-Out (Melaleuca's formulation = 250mg Mg amino acid chelate + 30mg B6). IMPORTANT: Mela-Out is NOT melatonin. It's magnesium. Two completely different things.
supplements.why_i_take_it: Mag deficiency is the most under-diagnosed mineral gap in American men. It affects over 300 enzymatic processes including testosterone synthesis, sleep quality, and blood pressure. I was grinding my teeth before I got serious about PM magnesium.

brands.name: Melaleuca Mela-Out
brands.note: What I use — Mg amino acid chelate + B6. Not melatonin.

brands.name: Doctor's Best High Absorption Magnesium Glycinate
brands.note: Best Amazon alternative — true glycinate form.

supplements.coach_note: Don't train on low magnesium. Every muscle contraction requires it. Most guys walking into a gym are already depleted.
supplements.doc_note: Glycinate form is the most bioavailable and least likely to cause GI issues. Citrate works but causes loose stools in higher doses. Oxide is essentially inert — avoid. The B6 in Mela-Out supports serotonin synthesis, which aids sleep quality independent of the Mg.

supplements.name: Omega-3 Fish Oil
supplements.timing: AM with fat (or split AM/PM with meals)
supplements.dose: 1–2g EPA+DHA combined (not total fish oil — read the EPA/DHA line specifically)
supplements.my_protocol: Included in the Melaleuca Peak Performance Pack. I've taken this for years. If you're eating salmon 3x a week you can probably skip a day, but for most people this is essential.
supplements.why_i_take_it: My 2018 lipid panel showed borderline-high triglycerides (159 mg/dL). Omega-3 is one of the few supplements with A-level evidence for reducing triglycerides. Also critical for joint health — which matters when you're doing slow-rep resistance training past 45.

brands.name: Melaleuca Peak Performance Pack
brands.note: Included — good quality, enteric coated to prevent fishy burps.

brands.name: Nordic Naturals Ultimate Omega
brands.note: Gold standard standalone. Third-party tested, high EPA/DHA concentration.

supplements.coach_note: Joint health is performance. Don't wait until your knees hurt to start Omega-3.
supplements.doc_note: His triglyceride trend was up across 3 consecutive panels (2013–2018). Omega-3 at 2g EPA+DHA daily has consistent evidence for 15–20% triglyceride reduction. Read the EPA+DHA label — a '1000mg fish oil' capsule might only contain 300mg EPA+DHA.

supplements.name: Creatine Monohydrate
supplements.timing: Daily — timing matters less than consistency. I take it AM in my shake.
supplements.my_protocol: Every day in my morning protein shake. Non-negotiable. The most-researched performance supplement in existence. Creatine monohydrate — don't pay premium for HCl or ethyl ester, monohydrate is the standard.
supplements.why_i_take_it: I'm 47, doing fit20 once a week, trying to hold lean mass while cutting visceral fat. Creatine is the one supplement with consistent evidence for lean mass preservation in older men. Also emerging research on cognitive function, which matters when you're running 5 businesses.

brands.name: Nutricost Creatine Monohydrate
brands.note: What I use. Clean, affordable, third-party tested. No frills.

brands.name: Melaleuca Sustain Creatine
brands.note: Good if you're already a Melaleuca member — convenient bundle.

supplements.coach_note: This is not optional for anyone training past 40. Period. The lean-mass preservation data is too strong to ignore. 5g daily, no cycling, no loading.
supplements.doc_note: Strong evidence for phosphocreatine resynthesis, lean mass maintenance in aging populations, and cognitive function (emerging). Kidney concerns are unfounded in healthy individuals at normal doses. Safe indefinitely.

supplements.name: Probiotic
supplements.timing: AM — or skip if you've had kombucha/Olipop that day
supplements.my_protocol: Daily unless I've had a probiotic-rich food (kombucha, kefir) that day. Gut health is the foundation everything else builds on — absorption, inflammation, mood, immune response.
supplements.why_i_take_it: I spent years in environments where gut health took a beating — deployments, stress, inconsistent diet. Probiotic is part of the long-game maintenance stack.

brands.name: Melaleuca Peak Performance Pack
brands.note: Included — Lactobacillus + Bifidobacterium blend.

brands.name: Garden of Life Dr. Formulated Probiotics
brands.note: Best standalone — 50 billion CFU, 16 strains, refrigerated.

supplements.coach_note: You can't out-train a leaky gut. Probiotic + prebiotic fiber is part of my nutrition prescription for every client.
supplements.doc_note: Most important in the context of his stress load and NSAID use (Mobic daily). NSAIDs disrupt gut microbiome — probiotic partially offsets this. Strain diversity matters more than raw CFU count.

supplements.name: Boron
supplements.timing: AM — part of T-stack on training days
supplements.my_protocol: Included in the Melaleuca Peak Performance Pack + additional on T-stack days. Boron is the sleeper supplement most men over 40 have never heard of. It's in my Core 7 because the research on free testosterone and SHBG reduction is compelling.
supplements.why_i_take_it: Boron reduces sex-hormone-binding globulin (SHBG), which means more of your total testosterone becomes bioavailable (free). At 47, total T might look fine on paper but free T can still be tanked by high SHBG.

brands.name: Melaleuca Peak Performance Pack
brands.note: Included.

brands.name: Pure TheraPro Rx Boron Glycinate
brands.note: What I add on T-stack days. Glycinate form.

supplements.coach_note: If you're over 40 and haven't had your free T tested, you're flying blind. Boron is a low-cost, low-risk way to support free testosterone.
supplements.doc_note: A 2015 study showed boron supplementation at 6mg/day for one week significantly increased free testosterone by 25% and reduced estradiol. Mechanism is SHBG reduction. Evidence is not as deep as creatine but the cost-risk profile is favorable.

supplements.name: Tongkat Ali (Longjack)
supplements.timing: AM — Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri/Sat only. Off Wed + Sun.
supplements.my_protocol: I use Carlyle brand. Part of my T-optimization protocol — never stack with Maca on the same day, and run it 5 days on, 2 days off (never stack with Ashwagandha — more on that below).
supplements.why_i_take_it: Tongkat Ali is one of the few adaptogenic herbs with actual clinical evidence for testosterone support in aging men — not just anecdote. At 47 with a high cortisol load (5 businesses + ministry + family), it's a tool for keeping T from being suppressed by stress.

brands.name: Carlyle Tongkat Ali 200:1 Extract
brands.note: What I use. 200:1 extract standardized. Good value.

supplements.coach_note: This is for men over 35 dealing with high-stress lifestyles. It won't turn you into a 25-year-old. It'll help you function like 40 instead of 55.
supplements.doc_note: 2022 RCT showed significant improvements in testosterone, muscle strength, and erectile function in men 50+. Run 5 days on / 2 days off. Do NOT stack with Ashwagandha — they compete on cortisol pathways and may blunt each other's effect.

supplements.name: Collagen Peptides
supplements.timing: AM with Vitamin C (enhances collagen synthesis)
supplements.my_protocol: Morning routine. At 47, connective tissue recovery is slower than it was at 27. Tendons, ligaments, and joint cartilage don't repair themselves the same way after 40. Collagen + Vitamin C is a cheap daily insurance policy.
supplements.why_i_take_it: I do slow-rep resistance training once a week — which puts a lot of time-under-tension on connective tissue. The cumulative benefit over years is real. Also good for skin and hair (side benefit, not primary reason).

brands.name: Vital Proteins Collagen Peptides
brands.note: What I use. Dissolves cleanly in coffee.

brands.name: Orgain Collagen Peptides
brands.note: Budget alternative — still quality.

supplements.coach_note: Take with Vitamin C — it's required for collagen synthesis. Collagen alone without the co-factor is less effective.
supplements.doc_note: Evidence is strongest for joint pain reduction and skin elasticity. Take within 1 hour of training for maximum collagen synthesis in connective tissue (recent research suggests the post-training window matters).

supplements.name: Glucosamine + Chondroitin + MSM
supplements.my_protocol: Daily — part of my AM protocol. Long-term joint maintenance. Not a quick fix. Takes 8–12 weeks to feel a difference. I consider this a years-long investment, not a supplement you evaluate in a month.
supplements.why_i_take_it: Two decades of Marine Corps service means accumulated wear on knees, hips, and spine. Glucosamine-chondroitin is the most studied joint supplement in the world. MSM adds anti-inflammatory support.

brands.name: Kirkland Signature Glucosamine + Chondroitin + MSM
brands.note: Best value. USP verified. Same quality as prescription brands at 1/10 the price.

supplements.coach_note: Marines do more damage to their joints in 20 years than most people do in a lifetime. This isn't optional for a veteran athlete over 40.
supplements.doc_note: GAIT trial evidence is mixed for knee OA but consistent for slowing cartilage degradation. The combination is more effective than each alone. Meaningful symptom difference typically appears at 8–12 weeks minimum.

supplements.name: L-Citrulline
supplements.my_protocol: Pre-fit20. I want maximum blood flow during a 20-minute all-out session. L-citrulline converts to arginine in the kidneys more effectively than arginine directly, driving better nitric oxide production and muscle pumps.
supplements.why_i_take_it: fit20 protocol is time-under-tension to true fatigue. Better blood flow = better nutrient delivery = better performance in those 20 minutes. Also supports cardiovascular health with long-term use.

brands.name: Melaleuca Sustain Pre-Workout
brands.note: Contains L-citrulline — convenient.

brands.name: Bulk Supplements L-Citrulline Malate
brands.note: Best value raw powder. 2:1 ratio.

supplements.coach_note: If you only add one pre-workout ingredient, make it citrulline. The pump isn't just aesthetic — it's nutrient delivery. At fit20 intensity you want every advantage in that 20-minute window.
supplements.doc_note: Citrulline → arginine → nitric oxide (NO) pathway. More effective than direct arginine supplementation due to first-pass metabolism. Also has solid evidence for blood pressure reduction over time.

supplements.name: Protein Shake (The Morning Stack)
supplements.dose: Target 60–80g protein per shake from multiple sources
supplements.my_protocol: Jocko Mölk (primary) + Levels Whey (secondary) + Mt. Athos Goat Whey (tertiary) + creatine + leucine + glutamine + MCT oil + cold brew coffee + coconut milk. I call it the Massive Protein Shake. Hits ~80g protein before I'm out the door. My 170g daily target starts here.
supplements.why_i_take_it: 170g protein per day is non-negotiable at my body weight for lean mass preservation during fat loss. A morning shake gives me ~47% of that target before 8am. Without it, I'm chasing protein all day and usually falling short.

brands.name: Jocko Mölk Protein
brands.note: Primary source. Clean profile, good amino spectrum.

brands.name: Levels Whey Protein
brands.note: Secondary blend. Mixes clean.

brands.name: Mt. Athos Goat Whey
brands.note: Tertiary. Easy on digestion if dairy is a problem.

supplements.coach_note: Multi-source protein gives you a better amino acid release curve than a single source. Whey is fast; goat whey + casein components extend the window. The creatine + leucine in the shake ensures both are in the muscle cell when protein synthesis is highest.
supplements.doc_note: At 47 with a target of visceral fat reduction, protein is the macro that does the most work — it preserves lean mass in a deficit, keeps satiety high, and has the highest thermic effect. 170g/day at his bodyweight (1.7g per pound of goal body weight) is correct.

supplements.name: Ginseng + Ginkgo Biloba
supplements.my_protocol: Rotating in on Wednesday and Friday. These are cognitive-support cycling supplements — not daily because tolerance builds. The Wed/Fri rotation keeps them effective.
supplements.why_i_take_it: Running 5 businesses, leading a ministry, training clients, and studying for NASM certifications. Cognitive edge matters. These are the most-studied nootropic herbs in the world — not hype.

brands.name: Nature's Bounty Ginseng Complex
brands.note: Good starter combo.

brands.name: Nature's Bounty Ginkgo Biloba 120mg
brands.note: Standardized extract — the form that actually works.

supplements.coach_note: The cognitive load on a guy running 5 businesses is as real as the physical load. These aren't optional extras — they're performance support for the mind.
supplements.doc_note: Ginkgo has Cochrane review support for mild cognitive impairment. Ginseng has adaptogenic evidence for stress resilience. Both show synergy for blood flow — which benefits both brain and peripheral circulation. Don't take Ginkgo with blood thinners.

supplements.name: Maca Root
supplements.timing: Friday AM only. Do NOT stack with Tongkat Ali on the same day.
supplements.my_protocol: Friday only. Maca and Tongkat Ali don't play well together on the same day — they compete on the same hormone-optimization pathways. Friday is Maca day. Stack with Ginkgo on Fridays.
supplements.why_i_take_it: Libido support, mood elevation, and mild hormone support without directly influencing testosterone like Tongkat does. It's an adaptogen that works more indirectly. Good for the Friday mental reset going into the weekend.

brands.name: Navitas Organics Maca Powder
brands.note: Gelatinized — better absorption. Mixes in shakes.

brands.name: The Maca Team Black Maca
brands.note: Black maca has the strongest evidence for libido/energy.

supplements.coach_note: Maca is mild but consistent. Friday is a good day for it — slightly elevated mood going into the weekend.
supplements.doc_note: Multiple RCTs for sexual function and mood in men 40+. Mechanism not fully understood but does not appear to directly raise serum testosterone (unlike Tongkat). Safe for long-term use. Gelatinized form is significantly better absorbed than raw.

supplements.name: CoQ10 (Ubiquinol)
supplements.my_protocol: Friday cycling supplement. CoQ10 is naturally produced by the body but declines significantly after 40. If you're experiencing any cardiac medication side effects (statins deplete CoQ10) this is non-negotiable.
supplements.why_i_take_it: Mitochondrial energy production and cardiac support. My 2018 lipid panel was concerning enough that I take cardiovascular health seriously. CoQ10 supports the heart muscle specifically.

brands.name: Qunol Mega CoQ10 Ubiquinol 100mg
brands.note: Best value ubiquinol form. Water + fat soluble = better absorption.

supplements.coach_note: If you're over 45 and not taking CoQ10 periodically, you're leaving mitochondrial health on the table.
supplements.doc_note: Ubiquinol (reduced form) is 2–8x more bioavailable than ubiquinone. Essential if on statin therapy (statins deplete CoQ10). At his age and lipid profile, this is worth having in rotation. Take with food — fat-soluble.

supplements.name: Beet Root Powder
supplements.timing: Wednesday AM (or pre-workout if training on a Wednesday)
supplements.my_protocol: Wednesday cycling supplement. Nitrate-rich beet root supports blood flow and endurance the same way L-citrulline does, through the nitric oxide pathway. Wednesday helps bridge the gap between Friday and Tuesday workouts.
supplements.why_i_take_it: Cardiovascular health + blood pressure support + performance. Multiple pathways to nitric oxide production means more redundancy in my vascular support stack.

brands.name: BioSchwartz Beet Root Powder
brands.note: What I use. High nitrate concentration.

supplements.coach_note: Beet root is the pre-workout choice for guys who don't want stimulants. Pure vasodilator — no caffeine jitters, just blood flow.
supplements.doc_note: Multiple RCTs show significant reduction in systolic blood pressure (average -4–5 mmHg) with daily beet root. His 2018 Chol/HDL ratio of 5.0 puts cardiac health in the watchlist category — this is worth keeping in rotation.

supplements.name: Greens Powder (Jocko Greens)
supplements.my_protocol: 3x per week when my vegetable intake has been low. I use Jocko Greens — fits the brand. Not a replacement for real vegetables. A bridge when you're eating on the go.
supplements.why_i_take_it: My diet is high-protein with variable vegetable intake depending on the week's pace. Greens powder closes the micronutrient gap on busy days. Fredericksburg traffic is real — some days it's a Chick-fil-A Cobb Salad or nothing.

brands.name: Jocko Greens
brands.note: What I use. Clean profile, no added sugars.

supplements.coach_note: Greens powder is not a vegetable substitute. It's a micronutrient insurance policy for when life doesn't cooperate with meal planning.
supplements.doc_note: Useful as a micronutrient bridge — not a replacement for whole vegetables. Most greens powders have good antioxidant and fiber content. Look for no added sugars and a clean label.

supplements.name: Ashwagandha (KSM-66)
supplements.timing: PM only, high-stress days only — 3–5x per week MAX. NEVER daily.
supplements.my_protocol: High-stress days only, never daily, never stacked with Tongkat Ali or Maca. Evening use only. This one has a hard warning: daily ashwagandha can blunt libido and dopamine drive over time. It works — but only if you cycle it correctly.
supplements.why_i_take_it: On high-cortisol days (back-to-back client sessions + C5iSR issues + family stress), ashwagandha in the evening is a legitimate cortisol reset. The science is strong. The daily abuse of it is what I want to warn you against.

brands.name: KSM-66 Ashwagandha by Jarrow
brands.note: KSM-66 is the only standardized extract with strong clinical backing. Don't buy generic.

supplements.coach_note: Use it like a tool, not a habit. High-stress day? Take it PM. Regular day? Don't. The difference between medicine and poison is dose and frequency.
supplements.doc_note: Multiple RCTs for cortisol reduction, stress, and sleep quality. The concern with daily use is blunted dopaminergic drive and reduced libido — this is anecdotally reported and physiologically plausible. PM dosing reduces cortisol without disrupting AM hormone pulsatility. KSM-66 only — other extracts have inconsistent evidence.

supplements.name: Daily Ashwagandha (Every Day Use)
supplements.my_protocol: This is on the AVOID list — not ashwagandha itself, but the pattern of taking it every single day.
supplements.why_i_avoid_it: Daily ashwagandha blunts libido and dopamine drive over time. It's a powerful adaptogen — which means it can down-regulate the systems it's supposed to support if you don't cycle it. I use ashwagandha situationally (PM on high-stress days, 3-5x/week max), but I took it off my daily stack.
supplements.coach_note: This is one of the most common mistakes I see: people take a supplement that works, assume more is better, and end up with blunted drive. Cycle everything.
supplements.doc_note: Anecdotal + physiologically plausible mechanism: prolonged cortisol suppression may reduce HPA axis responsiveness. The dopamine-blunting reports are consistent enough across user experience to take seriously. Use situationally.
supplements.avoid_reason: Daily use blunts libido and dopamine drive. Use situationally only (high-stress PM use, 3–5x/week max). See Ashwagandha KSM-66 entry for the right protocol.

supplements.name: HMB (Beta-Hydroxy Beta-Methylbutyrate)
supplements.my_protocol: Removed from my stack. Not worth the cost.
supplements.why_i_avoid_it: HMB is marketed heavily for lean mass preservation in older adults. The independent evidence (not funded by manufacturers) is underwhelming — especially compared to creatine monohydrate, which does the same job better and costs a fraction of the price.
supplements.coach_note: Creatine does everything HMB claims to do, with 30+ years of research behind it, at 1/5 the price. Don't let slick marketing substitute for real evidence.
supplements.doc_note: The initial Nissen et al. studies showed promise but were largely industry-funded. Independent meta-analyses show much smaller effect sizes. For lean mass preservation at this age, creatine monohydrate is the first-line supplement — not HMB.
supplements.avoid_reason: Underwhelming independent evidence. Creatine monohydrate is more effective and a fraction of the cost. Spend your money on creatine.

supplements.name: Horny Goat Weed
supplements.my_protocol: Tried it. Removed. Not worth the label space.
supplements.why_i_avoid_it: The mechanism (icariin → PDE5 inhibition) is theoretically interesting but the oral bioavailability is terrible. The active compound doesn't survive digestion well enough to produce meaningful systemic effects at normal doses.
supplements.coach_note: If the mechanism requires the compound to survive digestion and get into your bloodstream, and it doesn't, it's a placebo. Move on.
supplements.doc_note: In vitro data is interesting. Human RCTs are lacking and the existing studies are low quality. Bioavailability of icariin from oral supplementation appears insufficient for clinically meaningful PDE5 inhibition. Not recommended.
supplements.avoid_reason: Poor oral bioavailability — the active compound doesn't survive digestion at normal doses. The mechanism is interesting in theory; the delivery doesn't work.

supplements.name: Melaleuca Supplements vs. Amazon Subscribe & Save
supplements.my_protocol: My verdict after running both: I keep Melaleuca for HOUSEHOLD products (cleaners, personal care, Mela-Out, the Peak Performance Pack). I moved most standalone supplements to Amazon Subscribe & Save — same or better quality, 30–50% less.
supplements.why_i_avoid_it: Melaleuca charges a 2–4x price premium on supplements without a corresponding quality advantage. For example: Melaleuca Proflex Protein $32.24 vs Optimum Nutrition $35.50 (close), but Melaleuca Vitality multivitamin vs Nature Made is a significant markup. The household products are worth the Melaleuca membership. The supplements mostly aren't.
supplements.coach_note: Loyalty is fine. Paying double for the same molecule is not. Read labels. The Melaleuca Pack is worth it. Individual standalone supplements? Shop around.
supplements.doc_note: No meaningful clinical difference between Melaleuca supplement formulations and equivalent Amazon options in the same category. Use the membership value where it's real: household products, personal care, the Pack. Save the supplement budget for quality standalones.
supplements.avoid_reason: Not an avoidance of Melaleuca entirely — but a cost correction. Household + Pack = keep. Standalone supplements = usually better value on Amazon. Total estimated savings: $900–$1,500/year.
